mechanism_narrative: An increase in nonfarm payroll employment signals expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, which raises expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 52% of months with rising PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, restrictive monetary policy elevates borrowing costs and suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or the Sharpe ratio of the signal exceeds 0.0 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a federal funds rate above 4.5 percent and an unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals restrictive monetary policy hitting labor markets, which together erode corporate earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 45% of joint-trigger instances, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (incremental R-squared < 0.01) in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles identified by a federal funds rate above 4.5 percent, an industrial production index above 110 signals an overheating supply side that invites additional restrictive policy, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 55% of tightening-cycle months with INDPRO above 110, or the hit rate of the directional forecast falls below 45% in any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Advance retail sales below 350,000 million dollars signal insufficient consumer demand to support consensus revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months when RSAFS is below 350000, or the mean forward return during triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP exceeding 11,000 billion chained 2017 dollars confirms that aggregate demand is sufficiently robust to sustain corporate profit growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 45% of quarters when GDPC1 exceeds 11000, or the annualized forward return during these periods is below the risk-free rate.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous readings of industrial production above 110 and retail sales above 360,000 million dollars reflect a coordinated expansion across the supply and demand sides of the economy, which boosts near-term earnings sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails if positive 5-day returns occur in less than 55% of co-expansion months, or if either series alone produces a higher directional accuracy than the combined signal in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1,400 thousand units indicate a contraction in residential investment and construction employment that drags down aggregate demand and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances when HOUST is below 1400, or the mean forward return during triggered periods is positive.
